title: Water and Wastewater Treatment Plant and System Operators
description: Operate or control an entire process or system of machines, often through the use of control boards, to transfer or treat water or wastewater.

tasks.text: Collect and test water and sewage samples, using test equipment and color analysis standards.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Operate and adjust controls on equipment to purify and clarify water, process or dispose of sewage, and generate power.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Record operational data, personnel attendance, or meter and gauge readings on specified forms.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Add chemicals, such as ammonia, chlorine, or lime, to disinfect and deodorize water and other liquids.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Inspect equipment or monitor operating conditions, meters, and gauges to determine load requirements and detect malfunctions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured cognitive task involving analysis, inspection, or monitoring.

tasks.text: Direct and coordinate plant workers engaged in routine operations and maintenance activities.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Clean and maintain tanks, filter beds, and other work areas, using hand tools and power tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Maintain, repair, and lubricate equipment, using hand tools and power tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
